note:
当三星本月早些时候发布Galaxy Note 20时，最大的惊喜并不是更快的屏幕、更流畅的S Pen或更强大的处理器。这是三代Android更新的承诺，这对该公司来说是第一次。
现在，三星正在将这一承诺扩大到更多的手机。虽然该公司最初表示，这项保修只适用于其最高端的“S、N和Z系列设备，从S10开始”，但三星已经将其最新的A系列手机加入了这一名单，因此Galaxy A51和A71肯定会在2022年上市时获得Android 13。以下是完整的名单：
Galaxy S系列：Galaxy S20 Ultra、S20+、S20、S10、S10+、S10、S10e和S10 Lite。
这一保证还包括平板电脑回到Tab S6，以及上述机型的所有5G变种。三星表示，除了与手机一起发布的一次更新外，手机还将获得三次保证的Android更新。因此，从Android 11开始，Galaxy S10手机将获得对三个操作系统升级的支持。
这可是件大事。虽然用户希望1000美元的手机能获得多年的更新，但中档手机通常不是这样的。由于三星的A系列手机是其最畅销的设备，这意味着除了低价和高端功能之外，买家还有另一个理由购买这款手机。
这可能会给用户一个理由，让他们跳过谷歌价格实惠的新款Pixel4a。三星Galaxy A51的6.5英寸屏幕、四个摄像头和可扩展内存的售价为400美元，仅比谷歌最新的带有一个摄像头和5.8英寸屏幕的经济型手机贵50美元。与三星和其他安卓竞争对手相比，谷歌最大的优势是承诺三年更新，现在三星已经拉平了竞争环境，Pixel的买家可能会三思而后行。

note_en:
When  Samsung launched the Galaxy Note 20 earlier this month, the biggest surprise wasn’t the faster screen, smoother S Pen, or more powerful processor. It was the promise of  three “generations” of Android updates, a first for the company.
 Now Samsung is expanding the promise to even more phones. While the company originally said the guarantee would only apply to its highest-end “S, N, and Z series devices starting with the S10,” Samsung has added its latest A-series phones to that list, so the Galaxy A51 and A71 will be sure to get Android 13 when it arrives in 2022. Here’s the full list:
 Galaxy S series: Galaxy S20 Ultra, S20+, S20, S10, S10+, S10, S10e, and S10 Lite
 The guarantee also includes tablets going back to the Tab S6, as well as all 5G variants of the models above. Samsung says phones will get three guaranteed Android updates on top of the one that launched with their phone. So Galaxy S10 phones will "will get support for three OS upgrades starting with Android 11."
 That’s a big deal. While users expect thousand-dollar phones to get years of updates, that’s not usually the case for mid-range phones. And since Samsung’s A series phones are its best-selling devices, that means buyers have one more reason to pick one up other than their low price tag and high-end features.
 And it might give users a reason to skip  Google's affordable new Pixel 4a. Samsung's Galaxy A51 costs $400 for a 6.5-inch screen, four cameras, and expandable memory, just $50 more than Google's newest budget phone with one camera and a 5.8-inch screen. Google's biggest advantage over Samsung and the rest of its Android competitors has been the promise of three years of updates, and now that Samsung has leveled the playing field, Pixel buyers might think twice.

     Michael Simon covers all things mobile for PCWorld and Macworld. You can usually find him with his nose buried in a screen. The best way to yell at him is on  Twitter.
title: 三星承诺为许多Galaxy S、Note、Follible和A系列设备提供三代Android更新，这与谷歌对Pixel的三年承诺不相上下
title_en: Samsung promises three generations of Android updates for many Galaxy S, Note, Foldable, and A series devices, rivaling Google's three year promise for Pixel

note:
Filecoin是区块链界最受期待的项目之一，承诺将分散数据存储。
是的，但是：在幕后，投资者的反抗帮助推迟了大型揭幕仪式，并引发了关于谁拥有什么的法律调解。
重要原因：这是2017-2018年首次发行硬币热潮的混乱副产品，在那次热潮中，决斗帽子桌是一项功能，而不是一个错误。
背景：Filecoin的母公司Protocol Labs成立于2014年，参与了Y Combinator。
它后来筹集了几轮种子融资，包括来自BlueYard Capital、Digital Currency Group、Union Square Ventures和Winklevoss Capital的融资。这些投资明确是为了母公司的股权，在Filecoin项目中没有任何代币的承诺。
2017年，Filecoin通过象征性销售筹集了略高于2.05亿美元的资金，包括通过当时规模最大的ICO。一些(但不是全部)相同的投资者参与了进来。
阴谋：Protocol Labs保留了一定比例的代币，从理论上讲，这将是股票投资者应计的价值。
但多位消息人士称，这些代币中有很大一部分是首席执行官胡安·巴蒂斯-贝内特(Juan Batiz-Benet)单方面分发给自己和早期员工的，每人不到一便士，自那以后，他一直在继续分发这样的代币。
消息人士还表示，没有向股东分发代币，问题往往很难得到回答。
他们的意思是：我不认为胡安有恶意，他真心想让这件事取得成功，但他太年轻、太天真了，一位投资者表示。
另一位投资者补充道：你可能会认为，如果没有律师，这件事本可以解决，但实际上积聚了大量的敌意。
发生了什么：调解在过去一周内开始，似乎主要是通过股权投资者获得Filecoin代币(尽管没有他们想要的那么多)来解决。目前还不清楚是否所有的投资者都已经签署了这项拟议的决议。
很明显，它可能会失败，变得一文不值。但如果它成功了，它可能是巨大的，就像谷歌那样规模巨大，这就是为什么这变得如此有争议，一位不同的投资者解释说。
一句话：只要这场法律纠纷得到解决，Filecoin现在预计将在大约一个月后推出其主要网络。
note_en:
Filecoin is one of the blockchain world's most highly anticipated projects, promising to decentralize data storage.
 Yes, but: Behind the scenes, an investor revolt has helped delay the big unveil and sparked a legal mediation over who owns what.
 Why it matters: This is a messy byproduct of the 2017–2018 initial coin offering boom, in which dueling cap tables were a feature, not a bug.
 Background: Protocol Labs, the parent company of Filecoin, was founded in 2014 and participated in Y Combinator.
 It later raised a couple seed rounds, including from BlueYard Capital, Digital Currency Group, Union Square Ventures, and Winklevoss Capital. These investments were explicitly for equity in the parent company, without any promise of tokens in the Filecoin project.
 In 2017, Filecoin raised just over $205 million via token sales, including through what  at the time was the largest-ever ICO. Some, but not all, of the same investors participated.
 The intrigue: Protocol Labs retained a percentage of the tokens, which in theory would be value accruing to the equity investors.
 But multiple sources say that a large percentage of those tokens had been unilaterally distributed by CEO Juan Batiz-Benet to himself and early employees for less than a penny each, and that he has continued to make such distributions in the years since.
 Sources also say that no tokens were distributed to shareholders and that it often was difficult to get questions answered.
 What they're saying: "I don't think Juan was being malicious, and he genuinely wants to make this thing a success, but he was young and naive," one investor says.
 Another investor adds: "This is something that you'd think could have been worked out without lawyers, but there was a whole lot of ill-will built up."
 What's happening: Mediation began within the past week, and appears to be mostly resolved with the equity investors to receive Filecoin tokens (albeit not as many as they wanted). It's not clear if all investors have yet signed off on the proposed resolution.
 "Obviously it could fail and become worthless. But if it works it could be huge, like Google-scale huge, which is why this became so contentious," a different investor explains.
 The bottom line: Filecoin is now expected to launch its main network in about a month, so long as this legal dispute has been resolved.
title: 投资者反抗和法律纠纷推迟Filecoin计划
title_en: Investor revolt and legal dispute delay Filecoin plans

note:
根据一项新的研究，市场上最受欢迎的鱼类品种，如橙色粗糙鱼、普通章鱼和粉色海螺，都在世界各地迅速减少的鱼类和无脊椎动物物种之列。
在第一项此类研究中，UBC、GEOMAR Helmholtz海洋研究中心基尔和西澳大利亚大学的研究人员评估了1300多种鱼类和无脊椎动物的生物量-特定种群在水中的重量。他们发现，许多受欢迎的消费物种在全球范围内出现了一些严重的衰退。
在被分析的种群中，82%的种群被发现低于能够产生最大可持续产量的水平，这是因为被捕获的速率超过了可以再生的速度。其中，87个种群被发现属于“非常糟糕”的类别，其生物量水平不到最大限度地提高可持续渔业产量所需的20%。这也意味着，随着时间的推移，渔民捕获的鱼和无脊椎动物越来越少，即使他们捕捞的时间更长、难度更大。
“这是有史以来第一次对地球所有沿海地区被开发的海鱼和无脊椎动物种群生物量的长期趋势进行全球研究，”该研究的主要作者、UBC海洋与渔业研究所“我们周围的海洋”倡议经理玛丽亚·“邓”·帕洛马雷斯说。“当我们观察主要物种的种群在过去60年中的表现时，我们发现，目前它们的大部分生物量远远低于生产最佳捕获量的水平。”
为了得出他们的发现，研究人员将名为CMSY和BSMY的计算机密集型种群评估方法应用于我们周围海域在1950-2014年期间重建的海洋生态系统的综合渔获量数据。
种群降幅最大的是南温带和极地印度洋以及南极地大西洋，那里的人口自1950年以来减少了50%以上。
虽然全球大部分地区的鱼类和无脊椎动物呈下降趋势，但分析发现了一些例外。其中之一是北太平洋，其极地和亚极区的人口生物量增加了800%，温带的人口生物量增加了约150%。
根据合著者丹尼尔·保利(Daniel Paly)的说法，尽管有这些改善的地方，整体情况仍然令人担忧。丹尼尔·保利是我们周围海域的首席调查员。
保利说：“尽管有例外，我们的发现支持了之前的建议，即在过去的60多年里，世界上大部分地区的沿海和大陆架水域都出现了系统性和大范围的过度捕捞。”“因此，需要改善有效渔业管理的途径，这些措施不仅应该通过明确设定允许的年度总渔获量限制来推动，而且还应该通过执行良好和相当大的禁止捕捞海洋保护区来实现种群重建。”
“海洋生态区、气候带和海洋盆地中被开发鱼类种群的渔业生物量趋势”发表在“河口、海岸和陆架科学”上。
note_en:
Fish market favourites such as orange roughy, common octopus and pink conch are among the species of fish and invertebrates in rapid decline around the world, according to new research.
 In the first study of its kind, researchers at UBC, the GEOMAR Helmholtz Centre for Ocean Research Kiel and the University of Western Australia assessed the biomass—the weight of a given population in the water—of more than 1,300 fish and invertebrate populations. They discovered global declines, some severe, of many popularly consumed species.
 Of the populations analyzed, 82 per cent were found to be below levels that can produce maximum sustainable yields, due to being caught at rates exceeding what can be regrown. Of these, 87 populations were found to be in the “very bad” category, with biomass levels at less than 20 per cent of what is needed to maximize sustainable fishery catches. This also means that fishers are catching less and less fish and invertebrates over time, even if they fish longer and harder.
 “This is the first-ever global study of long-term trends in the population biomass of exploited marine fish and invertebrates for all coastal areas on the planet,” said Maria “Deng” Palomares, lead author of the study and manager of the  Sea Around Us initiative in UBC’s Institute for the Oceans and Fisheries.“When we looked at how the populations of major species have been doing in the past 60 years, we discovered that, at present, most of their biomasses are well below the level that can produce optimal catches.”
 To reach their findings, the researchers applied computer-intensive stock assessment methods known as CMSY and BSMY to the comprehensive catch data by marine ecosystem reconstructed by the Sea Around Us for the 1950-2014 period.
 The greatest declines in stocks were found in the southern temperate and polar Indian Ocean and the southern polar Atlantic Ocean, where populations shrunk by well over 50 per cent since 1950.
 While much of globe showed declining trends in fish and invertebrates, the analysis found a few exceptions. One of these was the Northern Pacific Ocean where population biomass increased by 800 per cent in its polar and subpolar zones, and by about 150 per cent in its temperate zone.
 Despite these pockets of improvement, the overall picture remains cause for concern, according to co-author Daniel Pauly, principal investigator at  Sea Around Us.
 “Despite the exceptions, our findings support previous suggestions of systematic and widespread overfishing of the coastal and continental shelf waters in much of the world over the last 60-plus years,” said Pauly. “Thus, pathways for improvements in effective fisheries management are needed, and such measures should be driven not only by clearly set total allowable annual catch limits, but also by well-enforced and sizeable no-take marine protected areas to allow stocks to rebuild.”
 “Fishery biomass trends of exploited fish populations in marine ecoregions, climatic zones and ocean basins” was published in  Estuarine, Coastal and Shelf Science.
title: 世界各地受欢迎的海鲜品种急剧减少
title_en: Popular seafood species in sharp decline around the world

德拉登(北阿肯德邦)[印度]，8月17日(ANI)：周一，在北阿肯德邦的甘戈特里国家公园(Uttarkashi)，森林研究所的知情人士发现了被认为在大约70年前灭绝的羊毛飞鼠。70年前，在IUCN红色名录中，毛飞鼠被认为已经灭绝。该研究所说，在一次调查中，我们在该州13个林区中的18个林区发现了这种松鼠。然而，位于德拉登的印度野生动物研究所的科学家们谈到了这种松鼠在巴格拉特山谷的存在，也发现了罕见的松鼠照片。森林研究所告诉媒体，毛茸茸的飞松鼠用它毛茸茸的爪子作为降落伞飞行。(ANI)。

Dehradun (Uttarakhand) [India], Aug 17 (ANI): Woolly flying squirrel, that was thought to be extinct some seventy years back, was spotted at Gangotri National Park in Uttrakhand's Uttarkashi, informed Forest Research Institute, on Monday. "The Woolly flying squirrel was considered extinct 70 years ago in the IUCN Red List. We spotted this squirrel in 18 of the 13 forest divisions of the state during a survey," said the institute. However, the scientists of Wildlife Institute of India situated in Dehradun have spoken of the presence this squirrel in the Bhagirath valley, and rare photographs of the have also been found. "Woolly flying squirrel uses its furry claws as a parachute to fly," Forest Research Institute told media. (ANI)

note:
铁锈项目最初是在2010年构思的(根据你的计算方式，你甚至可以说是2006年！)。作为Mozilla研究项目，但长期目标一直是将Rust建立为一个自给自足的项目。2015年，随着Rust 1.0的推出，Rust确立了独立于Mozilla组织的项目方向和治理。从那时起，Rust一直作为一个独立的组织运作，Mozilla是一个突出的、始终如一的财务和法律赞助商。
对于Rust语言得到业界许多公司的广泛使用和支持的机会，Mozilla一直并将继续感到兴奋。今天，许多公司，无论大小，都在以更多样化、更重要的方式使用Rust，从亚马逊的Firecracker，到Fastly的Lucet，再到为Discord、Cloudflare、Figma、1Password等提供动力的关键服务。
2020年8月11日，星期二，Mozilla宣布他们决定重组公司，裁员约250人，其中包括Rust项目和Rust社区的活跃成员。可以理解的是，这些裁员对铁锈项目本身的影响产生了很大的不确定性和混乱。我们在这篇文章中的目标就是解决这些问题。我们还有一件大事要宣布，请继续阅读！
不可否认，这些裁员对铁锈社区的所有成员都产生了影响，特别是那些在全球大流行中失去工作的人。突如其来的、意想不到的裁员可能是一次艰难的经历，当感觉到全世界都在关注时，他们的困难并没有减少。正在寻找工作帮助的受影响员工可以在Mozilla的人才目录中找到。
尽管铁锈项目对个人影响很深，但作为一个整体，它对此类事件的适应能力很强。我们有来自不同背景和雇主的不同群体的领导者和贡献者，而多样性是一种至关重要的力量。此外，人们普遍误解参加Rust Leadership的所有Mozilla员工都是这样做的，这是他们工作的一部分。事实上，Rust Leadership的许多Mozilla员工在他们的个人时间里为Rust做出了贡献，而不是作为他们工作的一部分。
最后，我们想强调的是，铁锈团队的会员资格是给个人的，与雇主无关。同时也是铁锈团队成员的Mozilla员工今天仍然是成员，即使他们受到了裁员的影响。当然，有些人可能会选择缩减他们的参与。我们理解不是每个人都能继续贡献，我们将全力支持他们的决定。我们对他们到目前为止为这个项目所做的一切表示感谢。
随着项目规模、采用率和成熟度的增长，我们开始感受到成功的痛苦。我们已经制定了法律和财政需求，而我们目前的组织缺乏能力来满足这些需求。虽然我们在Mozilla的帮助下成功了很长一段时间，但我们已经到了一个很难在没有合法名称、地址和银行账户的情况下运营的地步。“铁锈项目是怎么签约的？”已经成为我们不能再拖延的问题了。
去年，我们开始研究创建一个独立的铁锈基金会的想法。在开源基金会方面有过经验的Rust团队成员聚集在一起，查看当前的情况，确定我们需要从基金会获得的东西，评估我们的选择，并采访来自其他基金会的主要成员和主管。
在这项工作的基础上，Mozilla和Rust Core团队很高兴地宣布了创建Rust Foundation的计划。我们的目标是在今年年底前完成基金会的第一次迭代并投入运行。
这个基金会的第一个任务将是拉斯特已经很擅长的事情：取得所有权。这一次，资源是合法的，而不是程序中的东西。与Rust、Cargo和crates.io相关的各种商标和域名将进入基金会，基金会还将为它们所产生的费用承担财务责任。我们认为这个基金会的第一次迭代只是个开始。发展基金会的作用有很多可能性，我们很高兴能在未来探索这些可能性。
目前，我们仍然专注于基金会最初的狭隘目标。作为直接的步骤，核心团队已经选择成员组成一个项目组，推动形成基金会的努力。期待看到来自该小组的后续博客帖子，其中包含有关该过程的更多细节和提供反馈的机会。在此期间，您可以向该组织发送电子邮件至Foundation@rust-lang.org。
虽然我们才刚刚开始建立基金会的过程，但在过去的两年里，基础设施团队一直在带头减少对任何一家赞助该项目的公司的依赖，并增加支持Rust的公司的数量。
这些努力相当成功，正如你在我们的赞助页面上看到的那样，Rust的基础设施已经得到了整个生态系统中许多不同公司的支持。随着我们合法地过渡到一个完全独立的实体，基础设施团队计划继续努力，以确保我们不会过度依赖任何单一的赞助商。
我们很高兴能通过建立一个基础来开始这个项目的下一个篇章。我们要感谢到目前为止与我们分享这段旅程的每一个人：Mozilla孵化了这个项目并支持他们创建了一个基金会，感谢我们的领导者和贡献者团队不断改进社区和语言，以及每个使用Rust创建了强大的生态系统的人，这些生态系统驱使这么多人参与到这个项目中来。我们迫不及待地想看看我们充满活力的社区下一步会做什么。
note_en:
The Rust project was originally  conceived in 2010 (depending on how you count, you might even say  2006!) as a  Mozilla Research project, but the long term goal has always been to establish Rust as a self-sustaining project. In 2015,  with the launch of Rust 1.0, Rust established its project direction and governance independent of the Mozilla organization. Since then, Rust has been operating as an autonomous organization, with Mozilla being a prominent and consistent financial and legal sponsor.
 Mozilla was, and continues to be, excited by the opportunity for the Rust language to be widely used,  and supported, by many companies throughout the industry. Today, many companies, both large and small, are using Rust in more diverse and more significant ways, from  Amazon’s Firecracker, to  Fastly’s Lucet, to critical services that power  Discord,  Cloudflare,  Figma,  1Password, and many, many more.
 On Tuesday, August 11th 2020, Mozilla  announced their decision to restructure the company and to lay off around 250 people, including folks who are active members of the Rust project and the Rust community. Understandably, these layoffs have generated a lot of uncertainty and confusion about the impact on the Rust project itself. Our goal in this post is to address those concerns. We’ve also got a big announcement to make, so read on!
  There’s no denying the impact these layoffs have had on all members of the Rust community, particularly the folks who have lost their jobs in the middle of a global pandemic. Sudden, unexpected layoffs can be a difficult experience, and they are made no less difficult when it feels like the world is watching. Impacted employees who are looking for job assistance can be found on  Mozilla’s talent directory.
 Notwithstanding the deep personal impact, the Rust project as a whole is very resilient to such events. We have leaders and contributors from a diverse set of different backgrounds and employers, and that diversity is a critical strength. Further, it is a common misconception that all of the Mozilla employees who participated in Rust leadership did so as a part of their employment. In fact, many Mozilla employees in Rust leadership  contributed to Rust in their personal time, not as a part of their job.
 Finally, we would like to emphasize that membership in Rust teams is given to individuals and is not connected to one’s employer. Mozilla employees who are also members of the Rust teams continue to be members today, even if they were affected by the layoffs. Of course, some may choose to scale back their involvement. We understand not everyone might be able to continue contributing, and we would fully support their decision. We're grateful for everything they have done for the project so far.
  As the project has grown in size, adoption, and maturity, we’ve begun to feel the pains of our success. We’ve developed legal and financial needs that our current organization lacks the capacity to fulfill. While we were able to be successful with Mozilla’s assistance for quite a while, we’ve reached a point where it’s difficult to operate without a legal name, address, and bank account. “How does the Rust project sign a contract?” has become a question we can no longer put off.
 Last year, we began  investigating the idea of creating an independent Rust foundation. Members of the Rust Team with prior experience in open source foundations got together to look at the current landscape, identifying the things we’d need from a foundation, evaluating our options, and interviewing key members and directors from other foundations.
 Building on that work,  Mozilla and the Rust Core Team are happy to announce plans to create a Rust foundation. Our goal is to have the first iteration of the foundation up and running by the end of the year.
 This foundation’s first task will be something Rust is already great at:  taking ownership. This time, the resource is legal, rather than something in a program. The various trademarks and domain names associated with Rust, Cargo, and crates.io will move into the foundation, which will also take financial responsibility for the costs they incur. We see this first iteration of the foundation as just the beginning. There’s a lot of possibilities for growing the role of the foundation, and we’re excited to explore those in the future.
 For now though, we remain laser-focused on these initial narrow goals for the foundation. As an immediate step the Core Team has  selected members to form a project group driving the efforts to form the foundation. Expect to see follow-up blog posts from the group with more details about the process and opportunities to give feedback. In the meantime, you can email the group at  foundation@rust-lang.org.
  While we have only begun the process of setting up the foundation, over the past two years the Infrastructure Team has been leading the charge to reduce the reliance on any single company sponsoring the project, as well as growing the number of companies that support Rust.
 These efforts have been quite successful, and — as you can see on our  sponsorship page — Rust’s infrastructure is already supported by a number of different companies throughout the ecosystem. As we legally transition into a fully independent entity, the Infrastructure Team plans to continue their efforts to ensure that we are not overly reliant on any single sponsor.
  We’re excited to start the next chapter of the project by forming a foundation. We would like to thank everyone we shared this journey with so far: Mozilla for incubating the project and for their support in creating a foundation, our team of leaders and contributors for constantly improving the community and the language, and everyone using Rust for creating the powerful ecosystem that drives so many people to the project. We can’t wait to see what our vibrant community does next.
title: 为铁锈的未来奠定基础
title_en: Laying the foundation for Rust’s future

听着，我相信新的Microsoft Edge是一款非常棒的网络浏览器。我期待着有一天能试一试。
但是微软，我有一条建议给你：如果有这么多的Windows用户在谷歌上搜索“无法卸载Microsoft Edge”，以至于你觉得有必要完全拥有这个搜索结果，把它作为你的常见问题的标题……。也许不要一开始就把它强加给Windows用户？
需要说明的是，我并不是说微软不应该将用户更新到新版Edge，特别是在今天宣布从现在起整整一年后，Internet Explorer和旧版Edge将被完全淘汰之后。持续的安全更新非常重要！
但是，与其告诉用户什么可以有效地归结为“你不能卸载它，因为我们决定不让你卸载它”，或许微软可以接受一个暗示，给用户提供他们显然想要的东西。以下是近几个月来谷歌“卸载Microsoft Edge”搜索量的变化情况：
顺便说一句，显然你仍然可以摆脱新的Edge。(奇怪的是，微软的页面怎么会省略这一点。)。您只需熟悉几个shell命令即可。
note_en:
Look, I’m sure that  the new Microsoft Edge is a fantastic web browser. I’m looking forward to trying it someday.
 But Microsoft, I have a piece of advice for you: if so very many Windows users are googling “Can’t uninstall Microsoft Edge” that you feel the need to  utterly own that search result by making it  the title of your FAQ... maybe just don’t  force it on Windows users to begin with?
    To be clear, I’m not saying Microsoft  shouldn’t have updated users to the new version of Edge, particularly  after today’s announcement that Internet Explorer and legacy Edge will be fully phased out exactly one year from now. Continued security updates are important!
 But instead of telling users what effectively boils down to “you can’t uninstall it because we decided not to let you,” perhaps Microsoft could take a hint and give users what they apparently want. Here’s how Google search volume for “uninstall Microsoft Edge” has evolved in recent months:
  By the way,  you apparently  can still get rid of the new Edge. (Strange how Microsoft’s page omits that.) You’ll just have to be comfortable with a few shell commands.
title: 微软的“无法卸载Microsoft Edge”支持页面很能说明问题
title_en: Microsoft’s ‘can’t uninstall Microsoft Edge’ support page is hilariously telling

安全标志上要求的信息应安排为首先提供最重要的信息。关于与危险相互作用的后果的陈述通常当放在危险描述陈述之后或与危险描述陈述结合使用时最容易理解。作为一般规则，当有许多可行的行动/避免选择时，应首先发出危险信息。然而，当几乎没有避免选择时，行动/避免消息应该首先出现。
用标题风格写，去掉不重要的单词和代词(this，That，Thee)，冠词(a，the，an)和动词be(is，are，we)的形式。尽可能避免连字符。
行动说明为观众提供了如何避免危险的说明。陈述应简单、直接，并适用于危险。请勿进入！&#34；&#34；请勿操作&#34；&#34；戴安全帽&#34；&#34；禁止吸烟&#34；&#34；请勿操作&#34；&#34；
危险描述语句以清晰、简单的语言标识具体的危险。如果所需的行动和不避免危险的后果从危险描述陈述中明显可见(例如湿润时湿滑)，则可以省略行动和后果陈述。&#34；&#34；受限空间内的危险电压&#34；&#34；激光辐射&#34；&#34；热管&#34；
后果声明用清晰、简单的语言告诉观众，如果忽略警告将会发生什么。会灼伤，会导致严重烧伤或死亡。暴露在空气中会引起恶心、头晕和头痛等症状。
用主动语态而不是被动语态造句。这意味着把句子的主语放在第一位，动作(动词)放在第二位，宾语(名词)放在最后。通常可以从句子中推断出主语“你”或“你”是不必要的。
避免使用介词短语。介词短语通常可以去掉或用一个词代替。
文本的首选格式是大小写混合使用，其中只有句子中第一个单词的第一个字母是大写的。不鼓励将所有大写字母用于单词消息，因为它比小写字体更难快速阅读。有时，单个单词或短语可以用大写字母设置，以提供强调。

    APL (named after the book A Programming Language) is a programming language developed in the 1960s by Kenneth E. Iverson. Its central datatype is the multidimensional array. It uses a large range of special graphic symbols to represent most functions and operators, leading to very concise code. It has been an important influence on the development of concept modeling, spreadsheets, functional programming, and computer math packages. It has also inspired several other programming languages.

如果是这样的话，LastResort字体可能是有史以来最棒的字体。(对于蟑螂爸爸的粉丝来说，我很抱歉地说，这与世纪之交的nu-Metal经典毫无关系。)。
如果你是一个特别的Mac用户，这是一种只是偶尔在技术设置中出现的排版，当它出现时，它不会对你尖叫，部分原因是它大多数时候只显示一个字符-你试图使用的字体中缺少的字符类型。如果您所在的国家/地区的语言基于拉丁文字，通常是这样的：
但LastResort是一种比看起来更有趣的字体。它本质上是象形文字的排版形式，显示了一些不寻常的字符，旨在让人们在构建字体时有某种错误系统，帮助他们找出字体中可能缺少的东西。
LastResort不是一个容易挖掘的字体。它不会出现在Mac的表情符号界面中。当我从Unicode Consortium下载该字体进行分析时，我发现它没有显示在MacOS的默认字体书应用程序中。(如果要在那里查看，请下载不含任何小写字符的字体。)。下载第三方应用程序让我离看到它更近了一步，但即便如此，它也只显示了一个角色。
事实是，那里有很多字符，但你看不到它们，因为它们只是为了表示某种类型的字符而设计的。大多数用户看不到这些，因为他们基本上只使用一种语言。
但苹果，反映了其对可访问性的关注，必须对每个人都负责。例如，如果你进入Mac的/System/Library/Fonts文件夹，你可能会惊讶于里面有很多你可能从未使用过的有趣字体，其中之一就是LastResort。在我的基于Mojave的系统中，第一个显示的字体是“Apple盲文大纲6Dot.ttf”，与LastResort不同的是，这种字体很容易在Font Book中找到。许多其他语言也出现了。这些字体中的大多数都不是为您设计的；它们是为多语言操作系统必须考虑的任何微小边缘情况而设计的。
(但要小心那个文件夹：正如2005年MacWorld的一篇文章指出的那样，如果你从系统中删除少数几种字体中的一种，它就不会启动。)
LastResort是当所有其他边情况都已耗尽时出现的边情况。该字体最初是在1998年为MacOS 8.6开发的，最初是Apple Type Services for Unicode Image(ATSUI)堆栈的一部分，旨在为操作系统中出现的排版提供内部后备。
这些字形用作任何其他字体的备份；如果该字体不能表示任何特定的Unicode字符，则使用Last Resort字体中缺少的相应字形。这为用户提供了辨别它是哪种字符的能力，并为他们提供了正确显示字符所需的字体类型的线索。
(与之相比，如果你没有网站需要的某种字体，它会使用较差的备份字体，比如Times New Roman。)。
LastResort被屏蔽的轮廓字符的边缘反映了它的年代和年代，直到今天，它的数字和描述性字符仍然以芝加哥字体显示，这是Mac多年来独特外观和风格的标志性部分，但到20世纪90年代末，大多数苹果产品都停用了这种字体(iPod的早期版本是一个值得注意的例外)。
LastResort虽然有一个面向正面的用例，但主要面向开发人员，因为它让他们知道他们的Unicode兼容字体中可能缺少的字符。但即使是这样的用例，里面也有一些通常很奇怪的东西，包括一个土星形状的行星、一个外星人和一个鬼魂，以及对私人和未定义角色的引用。
甚至那些刻意想要有用的字符也很吸引人-阿兹特克象形文字出现了，字面上的埃及和玛雅象形文字，以及复活节岛的隆戈隆戈字形系统也出现了。如果它是一种类型的字符，那么它很可能出现在这个列表中。
但是，虽然ATSUI堆栈在MacOS中并没有持续下去，在十多年前被苹果目前的核心文本技术所取代，但LastResort仍然存在-实际上(恰如其分地)是由排版专家和语言学家迈克尔·埃弗森(Michael Everson)维护的Unicode标准的基础。
如果你去Unicode网站，事实上，你可以找到按LastResort代码排序的字符。
如果你想自己挖掘LastResort字符，Github上的用户altMind有值得一看的高分辨率字形渲染。里面有一些很野的东西。
签署“色情通讯”，即表示您同意接收来自“色情通讯”的电子通讯，其中有时可能包括广告或赞助内容。
note_en:
The thing they always say about good typography is that it often blends into whatever setting you’re using so it gets out of the way and you barely even notice it.
  If that’s the case, the font LastResort may be the greatest font of all time. (For fans of Papa Roach, I’m sorry to say it has nothing to do with  the turn-of-the-century nu-metal classic.)
  If you’re a Mac user in particular, it’s a piece of typography that only occasionally shows up in technical settings, and when it does, it doesn’t scream at you, in part because most of the time it only shows a single character—the type of character that is missing from the font you’re trying to use. If you’re in a country with a language based on Latin script, it’s generally this one:
   But LastResort is a more interesting font than it seems. It’s essentially the typography form of hieroglyphics, showing unusual characters intended for people building fonts to have some sort of error system that helps them figure out what might be missing from their typeface.
  LastResort is not an easy font to dig up. It does not appear in the Mac’s emoji interface.  When I downloaded the typeface from the Unicode Consortium to analyze it, I found that it didn’t display in MacOS’ default Font Book app. (If you want to see it there, download a font that doesn’t have any lowercase characters.) Downloading a third-party app got me a bit closer to seeing it, but even then, it only showed me a single character.
   The truth is, there are a lot of characters there, but you don’t see them because they are only designed to signify a certain type of character. Most users don’t see these because they largely stick to one language.
  But Apple, reflecting its focus on accessibility, has to account for everyone. For example, if you go into your Mac’s /System/Library/Fonts folder, you might be surprised at the sheer level of interesting fonts in there that you probably never use, one of which is LastResort. Buried inside my Mojave-based system, the first font that shows up is “Apple Braille Outline 6 Dot.ttf,” a font that, unlike LastResort, is easy to uncover in Font Book. Numerous other languages appear as well. Most of these fonts are not intended for you; they’re intended for whatever tiny edge case that a multilingual operating system must account for.
  (But be careful in that folder:  As a 2005  MacWorld  article notes, if you remove one of a handful of fonts from the system, it won’t boot.)
  LastResort is the edge case that appears when all the other edge cases have been exhausted. First developed in 1998 for MacOS 8.6, the font was initially a part of the  Apple Type Services for Unicode Imaging (ATSUI) stack, and was intended to offer  an internal fallback for typography that appears within the operating system.
   These glyphs are used as the backup of "last resort" to any other font; if the font cannot represent any particular Unicode character, the appropriate "missing" glyph from the Last Resort font is used instead. This provides users with the ability to tell what sort of character it is, and gives them a clue as to what type of font they would need to display the characters correctly.
  (Compare it to how,  if you don’t have a certain font that a website needs, it will use an inferior backup like Times New Roman instead.)
  Reflecting its age and dating it, the edges of LastResort’s blocked outlined characters, even to this day, feature numbers and descriptive characters displayed  in the font Chicago, an iconic part of the Mac’s distinctive look and feel for many years, but a font that was retired in most Apple products by the late 1990s (with a notable exception being the early versions of the iPod).
   LastResort, though with a front-facing use case, is largely intended for developers, as it lets them know characters that might be missing in their Unicode-compliant typefaces. But even though that’s the use case, there is some generally weird stuff in there, including a Saturn-shaped planet, an alien, and a ghost, along with references to private and undefined characters.
   Even the characters that were intentionally meant to be useful are fascinating— Aztec pictographs make an appearance, as do literal Egyptian and Mayan hieroglyphs, as well as the Easter Island  rongorongo glyph system. If it’s a type of character, it’s probably represented in this list.
  But while the ATSUI stack didn’t last in MacOS, being replaced more than a decade ago by  Apple’s current Core Text technology, LastResort is still there—and is in fact (fittingly) the basis of a Unicode standard that is maintained by typographer and linguist  Michael Everson.
  If you go to the Unicode website, in fact,  you can find characters sorted by the LastResort codes.
  If you want to dig through the LastResort characters yourself, the user altmind on Github  has high-resolution renderings of the glyphs worth checking out. There’s some pretty wild stuff in there.
  By signing up to the VICE newsletter you agree to receive electronic communications from VICE that may sometimes include advertisements or sponsored content.
title: Apple Last Resort字体：没有其他字体可用时显示的象形文字
title_en: Apple Last Resort Font: Hieroglyphics That Appears When No Other Font Available

东京在公园里安装了两个透明浴室设施，让人们在决定使用它们之前先看看它们有多干净，然后它们就会变得不透明。
设计背后的建筑师坂茂说，卫生间是由彩色智能玻璃制成的，当隔间被锁住时，玻璃就会变得不透明。
他说，这可以让用户检查厕所的清洁程度，以及是否有人从外面使用厕所。夜里，这个设施像一盏漂亮的灯笼照亮了公园。
到目前为止，卫生间已经安装在该市的两个公园里：Haru-no-Ogawa社区公园和福町迷你公园。
这些设施是东京厕所项目的一部分，该项目正在与不同的设计师重新设计该市17个公共卫生间。
该项目是由日本非营利组织日本基金会发起的，该基金会表示希望建造任何人都可以使用的公共厕所。
它说，所有的设计都是每个人都可以使用的，不分性别、年龄或残疾，以展示一个包容性社会的可能性。
该基金会表示，日本被认为是世界上最干净的国家之一，其公共厕所比世界上大多数其他国家都要干净。
然而，该基金会表示，由于人们对公厕黑暗、肮脏、难闻和可怕的刻板印象，日本公厕的使用受到了限制。
为了消除这些对公厕的误解，日本基金会决定与涉谷市政府合作，翻新位于东京涉谷的17座公厕。
透明设施及其他一些设计，已由八月五日起供市民使用。
其他设计包括一座石砌建筑，这座建筑在公园里看起来很漂亮，还有一个卫生间，里面有供男人、女人和所有人使用的空间。

note_en:
Tokyo has installed two see-through bathroom facilities in public parks, allowing people to see how clean they are before deciding to use them, at which point they turn opaque.
 The bathrooms are made of colored "smart glass," which turns opaque when the stalls are locked,  said Shigeru Ban, the architect behind the designs.
   "This allows users to check the cleanliness and whether anyone is using the toilet from the outside," he said. "At night, the facility lights up the park like a beautiful lantern."
   The bathrooms have been installed in two of the city's parks so far: the Haru-no-Ogawa Community Park and the Yoyogi Fukamachi Mini Park.
 The installations are part of  The Tokyo Toilet project, which is redesigning 17 of the city's public bathrooms with different designers.
  The project was launched by  The Nippon Foundation, a Japanese nonprofit that said it wanted "to build public toilets that can be used by anyone."
 It said all of the designs were "accessible for everyone regardless of gender, age, or disability, to demonstrate the possibilities of an inclusive society."
   The foundation said that Japan was known as one of the world's cleanest countries and that its public toilets were cleaner than most others around the world.
 "However, the use of public toilets in Japan is limited because of stereotypes that they are dark, dirty, smelly, and scary," the foundation said.
  "To dispel these misconceptions regarding public toilets, The Nippon Foundation has decided to renovate 17 public toilets located in Shibuya, Tokyo, in cooperation with the Shibuya City government," it said.
 The see-through facilities, along with some other designs, have been available for the public to use since August 5.
   Other designs include a stone building that is designed to look good in parks and a bathroom with spaces for "men, women, and everyone."

这位亿万富翁于2011年联合创立了Social Capital，七年后，他将这家投资公司转变为一家科技控股公司。这并不是受雇于Social Capital的投资者所期待的-或者他们希望与Palihapitiya一起前进的方向-促使几乎所有人随着时间的推移跳槽。
帕里哈皮蒂亚在那次外逃之后公开表示，他不认为投资是一项团队运动，现在他正在效仿沃伦·巴菲特(Warren Buffett)的策略。他希望社会资本类似于伯克希尔哈撒韦公司(Berkshire Hathaway)，后者拥有业务，并持有苹果(Apple)和可口可乐(Coca-Cola)等公司价值10亿美元的股份。
Palihapitiya计划实现这一规模的一种方式是创建特殊目的收购公司，即SPAC。SPAC是一种空白支票公司，它筹集了一大笔现金，上市，然后与一家私人公司合并。这一系列深奥的步骤允许私营公司在没有传统IPO繁重工作的情况下上市。2019年，社会资本获得了17亿美元的现金和现金等价物，这要归功于它对Slake和维珍银河(Virgin Galaxy)的投资，前者进行了直接上市，后者通过SPAC上市。
但是，他用另一种更安静的策略来追求他自助餐般的野心呢？一个接一个地收购业务。
这位投资者告诉TechCrunch，他已经收购了Hustle，这是一家由Insight Venture Partners、谷歌的GV和Salesforce Ventures支持的初创公司。Hustle的联合创始人罗迪·林赛(Roddy Lindsay)在Palihapitiya的Facebook团队工作了十多年，他们在那里密切了解了对方。但并不是他们在Facebook的共同时间敲定了这笔交易。这是他们对短信将扼杀电子邮件的世界的共同愿景。
该公司是认为电子邮件将在几十年后不再成为现实的众多初创公司之一。如果是这样的话，企业将需要新的方式将用户转化为客户。因此，Hustle让企业以个性化的一对一方式与用户进行交流，理想情况下，转化率更高。
但这家初创公司真正的不同之处在于其毫不掩饰的战略，即不向共和党或共和党候选人推销产品。Hustle首席执行官史蒂文·皮斯(Steven Pease)说，“许多无党派客户使用我们的平台，但我们在考虑与公司价值观不符的组织时，确实会使用过滤器。”
今年，Hustle发出了超过10亿条短信。Palihapitiya说，该公司的年收入超过1000万美元，并补充说，该公司是盈利的。
长期以来，政客们一直利用技术来传播他们的信息(例如，以唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的Twitter为例)，并与他们的支持者进行交流。但是，随着美国2020年11月总统大选的临近，用来激活选民的直接面向消费者的政治技术让人感觉更有先见之明。
“无论是每个枪支管制的城镇，还是计划生育，还是民主党，都有成百上千的人？”这些客户将试图激活他们的客户基础，做各种事情，“Palihapitiya说。“我希望在未来20到30年内拥有这样一个平台。”
Hustle是Social Capital在过去三年中的第三笔收购。2018年，社会资本收购了一家医疗保健企业，该企业拥有围绕人类生理的数据库。去年，该公司收购了一家精神健康初创公司，该公司以基于软件的治疗为中心，跟踪用户的进展情况。Palihapitiya拒绝透露这两项投资的名称，理由是目前不让媒体报道这些投资的竞争优势。
他说：“我喜欢建立不明显数据链接的企业。”他指出，这与人工智能、机器学习和其他未来主义技术不同。
尽管他的SPAC回报可能会推动收购，但他表示，他的交易一直是通过个人资本筹集资金的。
Palihapitiya对Hustle的长期战略是围绕它创建一个帝国。他计划收购ARR在500万至1500万美元之间的辅助业务，并将其整合，“现在突然之间，你可以看到我们达到了数亿ARR。”
Hustle的交易在大约一周内完成。他说，用自有资本的永久资产负债表进行投资，让他比传统的风险投资公司更快地承保决策，这与投资者普遍反对风险投资的情绪一致。
他指出，瑞士信贷(Credit Karma)和Intuit的合并尚未完成。
Palihapitiya说：“我们仍在等待这笔交易。”“你知道，我自己写不出88亿美元的收购案。但我可以写一本50亿美元的书。“
note_en:
The billionaire co-founded Social Capital in 2011, and seven years later, he pivoted the investment firm into a technology holding company. The transition wasn’t one that the investors hired into Social Capital were expecting — or a direction in which they wanted to move with Palihapitiya — prompting virtually all of them to jump ship over time.
 Palihapitiya, who said publicly in the aftermath of that exodus that he doesn’t think investing is a team sport, is now  modeling his strategy after that of Warren Buffett. He wants Social Capital to be similar to Berkshire Hathaway, which owns businesses and holds billion-dollar stakes in companies like Apple and Coca-Cola.
  One way Palihapitiya plans to achieve that scale centers on creating special purpose acquisition companies, or SPACs.  SPACs are blank-check companies which raise a bunch of cash, go public and then merge with a private company. The esoteric series of steps allows a private firm to go public without the strenuous work of a traditional IPO. In 2019, Social Capital made  $1.7 billion in cash and cash equivalents, due to its investment in Slack, which staged a direct listing, and Virgin Galactic, which went public through a SPAC.
 But the other, quieter tactic he is using to pursue his Buffet-like ambitions? Acquiring businesses one by one.
  The investor tells TechCrunch that he has acquired  Hustle,   a startup  backed by  Insight Venture Partners, Google’s  GV and Salesforce Ventures. Hustle co-founder  Roddy Lindsay worked on Palihapitiya’s team on Facebook for over a decade, where they got to know each other closely. But it wasn’t their shared time at Facebook that sealed the deal. It was their shared vision of a world where text-messaging would kill e-mail.
 The company is one of many startups that think e-mail will no longer be a reality in a few decades. If that’s the case, then businesses will need new ways to convert users into customers. So, Hustle lets businesses communicate with users in a personalized one-on-one way with, ideally, higher conversion rates.
 But the startup’s real differentiator lies in its unabashed strategy to not sell to Republican parties or Republican candidates. Steven Pease, the CEO of Hustle, said that “many non-partisan customers use our platform, but we do apply a filter when considering organizations that are at odds with the Company’s values.”
 This year, Hustle sent out more than 1 billion text messages. The company has north of a $10 million annual revenue run rate, Palihapitiya said, adding that the company is profitable.
 Politicians have long leveraged technology to spread their message (take  Donald Trump’s Twitter, for example) and communicate with their supporters. But, as we approach the United States’ 2020 presidential election in November, direct-to-consumer political technology used to activate voters feels even more prescient.
 “Whether it’s every town for gun control, whether it’s Planned Parenthood, whether it’s the Democratic National Party, there’s hundreds and hundreds [of thousands?] of customers here that are going to try to activate their customer base to do all kinds of things,” Palihapitiya said. “And I would like to own such a platform over the next 20 to 30 years.”
 Hustle is Social Capital’s third acquisition in the past three years. In 2018, Social Capital bought a healthcare business that has a repository of data around human physiology. Last year, the firm scooped up a mental health startup that’s centered around software-based treatments and tracks how users progress. Palihapitiya declined to disclose the names of either investment, citing competitive advantages in keeping them out of the press for now.
 “I like businesses that build non-obvious data links,” he said, noting that it is unlike AI, machine learning and other futuristic technologies.
 Although his SPAC returns could fuel acquisitions, he says that his deals have been funded through personal capital.
  Palihapitiya’s long-term strategy for Hustle is to create an empire around it. He plans to acquire auxiliary businesses that see $5 to $15 million in ARR, consolidate them, and “now all of a sudden, you can see us getting to hundreds of millions of ARR.”
 The Hustle deal closed in about a week. He says that investing out of a permanent balance sheet of his own capital lets him underwrite decisions faster than a traditional venture capital firm, which lines up with the investor’s general anti-VC sentiment.
 He pointed to Credit Karma and Intuit’s merger that is  yet to close.
 “We’re still waiting for that deal,” Palihapitiya said. “You know, I couldn’t write an $8.8 billion acquisition myself. But I could write a $5 billion one.”
title: Chaath Palihapitiya的下一个大骗局

发射于美国东部时间上午10点31分(太平洋标准时间下午7点31分)从位于佛罗里达州卡纳维拉尔角空军基地的SpaceX发射场进行。其中还包括对创纪录的重复使用助推器的回收尝试，该助推器在SpaceX的“当然我仍然爱你”的浮动无人机着陆驳船上进行了第六次着陆(也是记录创造)。这种成功的复苏意味着助推器有可能再次使用，在未来再次打破今天自己创造的纪录。
今天的运载火箭还使用了重新飞行的整流罩，这是从SpaceX的第四次星链任务中回收并翻新后再次使用的。总体而言，这是SpaceX创始人兼首席执行官埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)目标中迄今最大的成就，即最终能够完全重用公司航天器的几乎每个方面进行撤退任务，这应该有助于大幅降低火箭飞行的总体成本。
至于Starlink，它似乎正朝着SpaceX计划在今年某个时候推出的测试版服务进展顺利，该服务将覆盖美国和加拿大的部分地区。最近，PCMag报道说，Ookla用于测量互联网连接速度的Speedtest网站已经看到了一些看似合法的Starlink服务结果，这很可能处于使用轨道上现有卫星的早期(可能仅限内部)测试模式。
今天的发射还包括一次发射回收尝试，在海上使用SpaceX的“酋长女士”和“树女士”飞船。天气和其他条件将决定那些试图抓住因降落伞减速从空中坠落的整流罩的人，还是试图在它们落水后从海中打捞起来，但我们将在获得信息后提供这方面的最新情况。
note_en:
The launch took place at 10:31 AM EDT (7:31 PM PDT) from  SpaceX’s   launch site at Cape Canaveral Air Force Station in Florida. It also included a recovery attempt for the record-setting reused booster, which performed a sixth landing (also record setting) at sea on SpaceX’s ‘Of Course I Still Love You’ floating drone landing barge. That successful recovery means that the booster can potentially be used yet again, breaking its own record set today once again in future.
 Today’s launch vehicle also used a re-flown fairing, which had been recovered from SpaceX’s fourth Starlink mission and refurbished to use again. Overall, it represents the biggest achievement yet in SpaceX founder and CEO  Elon Musk’s   goal of eventually being able to fully reuse nearly every aspect of his company’s spacecraft for retreat missions, which should help dramatically reduce the overall cost of rocket flights.
  As for Starlink, it appears to be progressing well towards SpaceX’s planned beta service launch sometime this year, which will cover parts of the U.S. and Canada. Recently,  PCMag reported that Ookla’s Speedtest site for measuring internet connection speeds has been seeing some seemingly legitimate results for Starlink service, which is likely in early (possibly internal only) testing mode using the existing satellites on orbit.
 The launch today also includes a launch recovery attempt, using SpaceX’s ‘Ms. Chief’ and ‘Ms. Tree’ ships at sea. Weather and other conditions will determine weather those attempt to catch the fairings as they fall from the sky slowed by parachutes, or whether they attempt to recover them from the sea after they hit the water, but we’ll provide an update on that aspect of the launch as information becomes available.
title: SpaceX使用创纪录的重复使用猎鹰9号助推器成功发射第11次Starlink任务
title_en: SpaceX successfully launches 11th Starlink mission using record-setting reused Falcon 9 booster
